OpenStreetMap

OpenStreetMap (OSM) is a vector-based basemap that provides detailed road networks, place names, and general geographic reference information.

Characteristics

  • Community-driven data
  • Rich labeling (streets, cities, landmarks)
  • Lightweight and fast rendering
  • Updated frequently

Best Use Cases

  • Navigation and orientation
  • Identifying roads and infrastructure
  • General-purpose mapping

Limitations

  • Limited visual realism
  • Not suitable for terrain analysis
  • Can become cluttered with many overlays

Suggested Images

  • City map with street labels
  • Road network example

basemap osm
